articles.title: Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say
articles.summary: An 11-year-old is reported to have been killed in an air strike while manning a checkpoint in Tehran.

articles.title: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war with Hezbollah ends
articles.summary: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

articles.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
articles.summary: The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

articles.title: Judge temporarily halts construction of Trump's White House ballroom
articles.summary: The Republican-appointed judge ruled that Trump was the "steward" of the White House, but not the "owner".

articles.title: Peacekeepers killed by roadside explosion in Lebanon, initial report finds
articles.summary: The deaths of the two Indonesian nationals came less than a day after another UN peacekeeper - also Indonesian - was killed in Lebanon.

articles.title: Italian Christmas meal tragedy turns into murder inquiry
articles.summary: Tests results suggest the two women may have been killed with ricin, according to Italian media, but police still do not have a suspect.

articles.title: China bans storing cremated remains in empty 'bone ash apartments'
articles.summary: China bans 'bone ash apartments' where mourners are storing cremated remains instead of cemeteries.

articles.title: Wolf bites woman in shock German attack on busy Hamburg shopping street
articles.summary: It is believed to be the first wolf attack on a human since the animals began to reestablish themselves in Germany decades ago.

articles.title: Eurovision Song Contest launches first-ever Asia edition
articles.summary: Broadcasters from 10 countries, including South Korea and the Philippines, will be taking part.

articles.title: Russian oil tanker docks in Cuba ending near-total blockade
articles.summary: A Russian oil tanker has docked in Cuba, providing temporary relief after months of shortages.
